data.description: Explore and produce a plan document at docs/c11mux-theming-plan.md for a unified theme engine that covers all c11mux chrome surfaces (sidebar, top bar, tab bar, dividers, outer workspace frame) while leaving Ghostty-owned surfaces untouched. Plan must cover current-state audit, workspace color audit, schema design, divider/frame primitives, migration, UX, phased rollout, non-goals, open questions, risks.
data.title: Custom theming system plan

data.body: Plan document drafted at docs/c11mux-theming-plan.md (1085 lines). Covers all 10 sections from the prompt: current-state audit, workspace color audit, C11muxTheme schema (TOML), divider + frame primitives, migration story, discoverability/UX, phased rollout (M1-M5), non-goals, open questions for human review, risks. Includes 3 built-in theme examples in Appendix A.

7 open questions locked 2026-04-18 before Trident review:
1. Default theme = Stage 11 brand (void-dominant, gold accent)
2. Frame thickness = 1.5pt
3. Inactive-frame opacity = 0.25
4. Tab-bar active indicator default = $workspaceColor
5. CLI namespace: `cmux themes` stays Ghostty (king theme); c11mux chrome under `cmux ui themes`
6. Per-workspace theme-file override deferred past v1 (M5 if asked)
7. TOML parser: hand-written subset (~200 lines, zero deps)

Plan updated to v1.1. Trident plan review command ready to issue next.
